items.content: Anthropic is debuting a new AI model as part of a cybersecurity partnership with Nvidia, Google, Amazon Web Services, Apple, Microsoft, and other companies. Project Glasswing, as it’s called, is billed as a way for large companies, and potentially even the government, to flag vulnerabilities in their systems with virtually no human intervention.Anthropic is offering its launch partners access to Claude Mythos Preview, a new general-purpose model that it’s not currently planning to publicly release due to security concerns. Newton Cheng, the cyber lead for Anthropic’s frontier red team, told The Verge that the model will ideally give cyber defenders a “head start” against adversaries. The partners will use the model to analyze their system to spot high-stakes vulnerabilities and help patch them up. Access is restricted to keep those same adversaries from using it to find weak points and conduct attacks.Though Claude Mythos Preview wasn’t specifically trained for cybersecurity purposes, Anthropic said in a release that the model’s “strong agentic coding and reasoning skills” are behind its cybersecurity advances. In an interview with The Verge, Newton Cheng, the cyber lead for Anthropic’s frontier red team, declined to share specific details of the model’s cybersecurity successes beyond the company’s publicly-released examples, but Anthropic’s blog post said that in recent weeks, Mythos Preview has flagged “thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities, including some in every major operating system and web browser.” Anthropic’s blog post doesn’t mention keeping humans in the loop for the model’s cybersecurity sweeps; in fact, it highlights that the model identified vulnerabilities “and develop[ed] many related exploits — entirely autonomously, without any human steering.”Claude Mythos Preview’s existence was first reported last month in a data leak, which Anthropic attributes to human error. Dianne Penn, a head of product management at Anthropic, told The Verge in an interview that the company is “taking steps in terms of solidifying our processes … That was not related to software vulnerabilities in any way.”Mythos Preview will be privately available to the company’s Glasswing partners, which also include JPMorgan Chase, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, the Linux Foundation, and Palo Alto Networks, plus about 40 other organizations that maintain or build software infrastructure. For now, Anthropic will help subsidize the cost of using it. The company says it will commit up to $100 million in usage credits, plus $4 million in direct donations to the Linux Foundation and the Apache Software Foundation, said Cheng. In the long term, as Anthropic and other AI companies face pressure to turn a profit, the program could evolve into a paid service that provides a new revenue stream — if it works well enough for companies to keep using it.Despite its highly public recent clash with the Trump administration, Anthropic also said in the release that it has been in “ongoing discussions with US government officials about Claude Mythos Preview and its offensive and defensive cyber capabilities.” When The Verge asked what that meant, Penn confirmed that the company had “briefed senior officials in the US government about Mythos and what it can do,” and that the company is still “committed to working closely with all different levels of government.” Cheng said that though Anthropic is “engaged with” the government, he declined to speak to exactly who the company had briefed.
items.author: Hayden Field
items.titleZh: 新型Anthropic模型发现"每项主流操作系统及网络浏览器均存在安全隐患"
items.descriptionZh:
Anthropic联合Nvidia、谷歌、亚马逊云科技（AWS）、苹果、微软等科技巨头，共同推出一项名为“Project Glasswing”的网络安全合作项目。该项目旨在为大型企业乃至政府机构提供近乎无需人工干预的系统漏洞检测与修复能力。其核心是Anthropic最新开发的通用大模型——Claude Mythos Preview。由于安全考虑，该模型目前不计划公开发布，仅对项目合作伙伴开放。

在技术原理与创新性方面，Claude Mythos Preview并非专门为网络安全训练，但其核心优势在于强大的“智能体编码与推理能力”。正是这些通用能力使其在网络安全领域展现出卓越性能，能够自主分析复杂系统代码和架构，识别潜在漏洞。最大的创新点在于其高度的自主性：根据Anthropic官方博客，该模型不仅能“标记数千个高危漏洞”，还能“完全自主地开发相关的漏洞利用程序”，整个过程无需任何人工引导。这标志着AI在网络安全领域从辅助工具向自主智能体的重要演进。

关于性能表现，尽管Anthropic未披露详尽的基准测试对比数据，但其宣称的成果颇具说服力。在近期内部测试中，Mythos Preview已成功标记了“成千上万个高危漏洞，覆盖所有主流操作系统和网页浏览器”。这种在广泛而关键的数字基础设施中主动发现漏洞的能力，为防御方提供了宝贵的“先发优势”。项目合作伙伴（包括摩根大通、博通、思科、CrowdStrike、Linux基金会、Palo Alto Networks等约50家维护或构建软件基础设施的组织）将使用该模型来分析自身系统，定位高风险漏洞并协助修补。访问权限的严格限制，旨在防止相同的强大能力被对手用于发动攻击。

此次合作具有深远的技术与行业影响。首先，它将可能重塑企业安全运维模式，实现从被动响应到主动、持续、自动化漏洞猎杀的转变。其次，它开辟了AI模型在高度敏感、高风险的网络安全领域商业化应用的新路径。在应用场景上，该项目直接瞄准大型企业和关键政府部门的核心系统安全防护，这是网络安全需求最迫切、价值最高的领域。

值得注意的是，该模型的存在此前因一次“人为错误”导致的数据泄露而被曝光。Anthropic表示已加强内部流程。在商业层面，为推广此项目，Anthropic承诺提供高达1亿美元的使用额度补贴，并向Linux基金会和阿帕奇软件基金会直接捐赠400万美元。长远来看，如果模型效果获得市场持续认可，该项目有望演变为一项为Anthropic带来新收入的付费服务。

此外，尽管Anthropic近期与前特朗普政府存在公开分歧，但其在新闻稿中确认，已就Claude Mythos Preview的“进攻与防御网络能力”与美国政府官员进行“持续讨论”，并向高级官员进行了技术简报，表明了其与各级政府保持合作的意向。这凸显了此类尖端AI技术在国家安全层面的战略意义以及随之而来的治理挑战。

items.title: Intel will help build Elon Musk’s Terafab AI chip factory
items.description:
Elon Musk's Terafab AI chip project in Austin, Texas, is gaining a crucial new partner: Intel.
On Tuesday, the American chipmaker announced it was signing on to help design and build the sprawling facility, which would supply AI chips to Musk's two companies, SpaceX (newly merged with xAI) and Tesla. Musk needs AI chips to power his plans to build a "robot army" that includes self-driving cars and humanoid robots, as well as for the data centers he plans on launching into space. SpaceX plans on making its initial public offering later this year. 
"Terafab will close the gap between today's chip production and the future's demand - a future …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: Elon Musk’s Terafab AI chip project in Austin, Texas, is gaining a crucial new partner: Intel.On Tuesday, the American chipmaker announced it was signing on to help design and build the sprawling facility, which would supply AI chips to Musk’s two companies, SpaceX (newly merged with xAI) and Tesla. Musk needs AI chips to power his plans to build a “robot army” that includes self-driving cars and humanoid robots, as well as for the data centers he plans on launching into space. SpaceX plans on making its initial public offering later this year.“Terafab will close the gap between today’s chip production and the future’s demand - a future among the stars,” the Terafab website reads.The inclusion of Intel relieves some of the pressure on Musk to build the factory himself. In recent months, the billionaire has sounded increasingly desperate about his desire for someone to build a chip factory, or fab, to help fuel his AI ambitions. And he has questioned whether the chip-making industry can keep up with the demand. “Can someone else build these things?” Musk said during an earnings call earlier this year. “I mean, it’s very hard to build these things.”Building a chip fabrication plant is complex, requires billions of dollars, many years, and a ton of specialized equipment. Musk has experience building factories for cars and rockets, but not silicon.Now, it seems like the factory’s construction will fall on Intel, which is dealing with its own struggles. “Our ability to design, fabricate, and package ultra-high-performance chips at scale will help accelerate Terafab’s aim to produce 1 TW/year of compute to power future advances in AI and robotics,” the company said in a post on X today.Intel is currently building two fabs in Arizona, as part of a $20 billion investment to grow its US footprint. Taiwan’s TSMC is also planning an enormous “Gigafab” north of Phoenix, with plans to operate as many as 12 advanced semiconductor plants.
items.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
items.titleZh: 英特尔将助力建造埃隆·马斯克的Terafab人工智能芯片工厂
items.descriptionZh:
埃隆·马斯克位于得克萨斯州奥斯汀的Terafab人工智能芯片项目迎来关键合作伙伴——英特尔。本周二，这家美国芯片制造商宣布将参与该大型设施的设计与建造，该设施未来将为马斯克旗下两家公司（SpaceX与新合并的xAI）及特斯拉供应AI芯片。马斯克需要这些芯片驱动其包括自动驾驶汽车、人形机器人在内的“机器人军团”计划，以及计划部署至太空的数据中心。SpaceX预计今年下半年启动首次公开募股。

Terafab官网宣称其将“弥合当前芯片生产与未来星际时代需求之间的差距”。英特尔的加入缓解了马斯克自主建厂的压力。近几个月，这位亿万富翁多次公开表达对建造芯片工厂（fab）以支撑其AI野心的迫切需求，并质疑芯片行业能否跟上需求增速。他在年初财报电话会议上坦言：“有没有别人能造这些？建造这些东西真的非常困难。”

芯片制造厂建设极其复杂，需投入数十亿美元、耗时多年，并依赖大量特种设备。马斯克虽具备汽车和火箭工厂的建设经验，但半导体制造领域仍是空白。如今，工厂建设重任将落在正面临自身转型挑战的英特尔肩上。英特尔今日在X平台发文称：“我们大规模设计、制造和封装超高性能芯片的能力，将助力加速实现Terafab年产1太瓦（TW）算力的目标，以推动AI与机器人技术的未来进展。”

目前英特尔正在亚利桑那州建设两座芯片工厂，作为其200亿美元美国本土扩张计划的一部分。与此同时，中国台湾的台积电也计划在凤凰城北部建造巨型“Gigafab”园区，规划运营多达12座先进半导体工厂。这一合作标志着传统芯片巨头与新兴AI算力需求方的深度结合，或将重塑高端芯片制造格局。

items.title: Intel joins Elon Musk's TeraFab project — 'Intel is proud to join the Terafab project with SpaceX, xAI, and Tesla to help refactor silicon fab technology'
items.description: As Intel joins Elon Musk's TeraFab project, Lip-Bu Tan expects Elon Musk to reimagine the semiconductor industry.

Subscribe to our newsletter In an unexpected turn of events, Intel on Tuesday said that it had joined Elon Musk's TeraFab project. The announcement mentions Intel's ability to develop, produce, and package advanced processors in high volumes, which could help Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI to get enough compute performance for next-generation AI and robotics applications. However, the announcement made in an X post you can expand below does not reveal how exactly Intel will help TeraFab.Intel is proud to join the Terafab project with @SpaceX, @xAI, and @Tesla to help refactor silicon fab technology.Our ability to design, fabricate, and package ultra-high-performance chips at scale will help accelerate Terafab’s aim to produce 1 TW/year of compute to power… pic.twitter.com/2vUmXn0YhHApril 7, 2026"Intel is proud to join the Terafab project with SpaceX, xAI, and Tesla to help refactor silicon fab technology," a statement by Intel reads. "Our ability to design, fabricate, and package ultra-high-performance chips at scale will help accelerate Terafab's aim to produce 1 TW/year of compute to power future advances in AI and robotics. It was fun hosting Elon Musk at Intel this past weekend!"The announcement is not accompanied by any press releases or SEC filings, which raises questions about the framework of the collaboration between Intel and TeraFab, as well as any possible legal bindings. In fact, the post in X is deliberately written in a way that barely reveals any concrete details about the structure of the partnership.Article continues below You may like Elon Musk unveils $20 billion ‘TeraFab’ chip project to make chips, memory, and package processors all under one roof Elon Musk says his Terafab Project chipmaking venture will launch in seven days Analyzing Elon Musk's TeraFab — A step towards Tesla and SpaceX's partial vertical integration, or an unattainable dream? Officially, TeraFab is positioned as the "most epic chip-building effort ever" that is to combine "logic, memory and advanced packaging under one roof," which implies localized production in a massive facility. Furthermore, the company is hiring managers to build a greenfield semiconductor fabrication plant in Texas. By contrast, Intel's wording rather implies a virtual semiconductor production ecosystem, or even a consortium that involves chip design, manufacturing, and packaging at Intel and demand from Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI. How such a consortium would differ from a typical wafer supply agreement that large companies tend to have with their suppliers is something that is unclear at this point.Considering the fact that Elon Musk wants his TeraFab project to ramp up production of chips as soon as possible, a reasonable way to achieve this would be to build a coordinated supply chain with a capacity pool that includes its own facilities as well as facilities belonging to other chipmakers, such as Intel. If we were to speculate further, we would envision custom co-invested fabs or at least product lines that would use unified process technologies, thus offering Elon Musk's companies dual or even triple sourcing to satisfy their demands.In fact, Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI may even order Intel to develop custom silicon tailored specifically for their workloads, as Intel officially offers custom silicon development services, though this is speculation.But while the Intel – TeraFab collaboration announcement lacks details on how Elon Musk plans to produce chips that would consume one terawatt of power on an annual basis, the chief executive of Intel expects Elon Musk to reimagine the entire semiconductor industry."Elon has a proven track record of reimagining entire industries," Lip-Bu Tan wrote in his own X post. "This is exactly what is needed in semiconductor manufacturing today. Terafab represents a step change in how silicon logic, memory and packaging will get built in the future. Intel is proud to be a partner and work closely with Elon on this highly strategic project."Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Anton Shilov
items.titleZh: 英特尔加入马斯克TeraFab项目——"英特尔很自豪能与SpaceX、xAI和特斯拉共同参与Terafab项目，助力重构硅芯片制造技术"
items.descriptionZh:
在一项出人意料的进展中，英特尔于周二宣布已加入埃隆·马斯克主导的TeraFab项目。该声明强调了英特尔大规模设计、制造和封装先进处理器（芯片）的能力，此举或将助力特斯拉、SpaceX及xAI获得足够的计算性能，以推动下一代人工智能与机器人技术的发展。然而，这份通过社交媒体X发布的声明并未具体阐明英特尔将如何协助TeraFab。

英特尔在声明中表示：“我们很自豪能与SpaceX、xAI和特斯拉共同加入TeraFab项目，助力重构硅晶圆厂技术。我们大规模设计、制造和封装超高性能芯片的能力，将有助于加速TeraFab实现年产1太瓦（1万亿瓦）计算能力的目标，为人工智能和机器人技术的未来进步提供动力。”声明还提到，英特尔上周末接待了马斯克到访。

此次公告并未伴随任何新闻稿或美国证券交易委员会文件，这引发了外界对双方合作框架及潜在法律约束的疑问。事实上，该社交媒体帖文措辞谨慎，几乎未透露任何关于合作结构的具体细节。

根据官方定位，TeraFab被描述为“有史以来最宏大的芯片制造计划”，旨在将“逻辑芯片、存储芯片和先进封装集成在同一屋檐下”，这暗示了其计划在一个大型设施中进行本地化生产。该公司正在得克萨斯州招聘管理人员，以建设一座全新的半导体制造工厂（晶圆厂）。

与之相对，英特尔的措辞更倾向于暗示一个虚拟的半导体生产生态系统，甚至可能是一个涉及英特尔芯片设计、制造、封装以及特斯拉、SpaceX和xAI需求的联盟。这种模式与大型公司通常与其供应商签订的晶圆供应协议有何不同，目前尚不明确。

考虑到马斯克希望TeraFab项目尽快提升芯片产量，一个合理的实现途径是构建一个协调的供应链，其产能池既包括自有设施，也包括英特尔等其他芯片制造商的工厂。进一步推测，这可能涉及共同投资的定制化晶圆厂，或至少是采用统一工艺技术的产品线，从而为马斯克旗下公司提供双重甚至三重供应来源，以满足其需求。

实际上，特斯拉、SpaceX和xAI甚至可能要求英特尔为其特定工作负载开发定制化硅芯片，因为英特尔官方确实提供定制芯片设计服务，尽管这仍属推测。

尽管英特尔的合作公告未详述马斯克计划如何生产年耗电量高达1太瓦的芯片，但英特尔首席执行官对马斯克重塑整个半导体产业寄予厚望。他在其X帖子中写道：“埃隆拥有重塑整个行业的成功履历，而这正是当今半导体制造业所需要的。TeraFab代表了未来硅逻辑、内存和封装制造方式的跨越式变革。英特尔很自豪能成为合作伙伴，并与埃隆在这一高度战略性的项目上紧密合作。”

总体而言，此次合作标志着传统半导体巨头与新兴垂直整合科技巨头之间的一次重要联手，其核心目标是通过整合设计与制造资源，突破现有算力规模瓶颈，为下一代AI与自动化系统奠定硬件基础。具体合作形式与商业模式仍有待后续披露。

items.titleZh: 苹果新款M5芯片MacBook Air 13寸降价促销，最高立省150美元，现仅售949美元起——全系机型迎来迄今最大力度优惠
items.descriptionZh:
苹果公司近期凭借其产品发布取得市场关注，继Neo系列的成功后，现针对2026年款的13英寸M5 MacBook Air笔记本电脑推出全系折扣促销，为新上市不到一个月的机型提供了高达150美元的降价，在当前经济环境下显得尤为诱人。这些折扣主要通过亚马逊平台提供，而官方Apple Store仍维持原价销售，使得此次促销成为消费者入手新款MacBook Air的绝佳时机。

新款MacBook Air搭载苹果最新的M5芯片，这是其核心技术创新点。该芯片采用10核CPU和10核GPU架构，相比前代M4芯片在速度和整体性能上实现了渐进式提升，延续了苹果自研芯片在能效比和集成度方面的优势。此外，笔记本内置苹果N1无线芯片，支持最新的Wi-Fi 7和蓝牙6连接标准，确保高速无线传输和低延迟体验。其他硬件配置包括13.6英寸Liquid Retina显示屏、12MP中心舞台摄像头和Touch ID指纹识别，这些特性共同提升了设备的显示效果、视频通话质量和安全性。

在性能参数和对比数据方面，新闻详细列出了三种主要配置及其折扣详情。首先是顶配机型，配备24GB统一内存和1TB SSD存储，原价1499美元，现价1349.99美元，节省149.01美元；其次是中配机型，配备16GB统一内存和1TB SSD存储，原价1299美元，现价1149.99美元，同样节省149.01美元；最后是入门机型，配备16GB统一内存和512GB SSD存储，原价1099美元，现价949美元，直降150美元。这些折扣使得各配置机型的价格分别降低了约10%至14%，性价比显著提高。所有机型均采用统一的轻薄设计，统一内存架构优化了多任务处理能力，而SSD存储选项则满足不同用户对空间的需求。

从技术影响和应用场景来看，M5 MacBook Air的轻薄特性使其成为移动办公和日常娱乐的理想选择。M5芯片的性能提升，结合Wi-Fi 7的增强连接能力，可支持更高效的内容创作、编程开发和多媒体消费。折扣促销不仅降低了消费者的入门门槛，还可能刺激市场对苹果新品的早期采纳，尤其是在经济不确定性增加的背景下。对于计划升级设备的用户而言，现在入手能以更低成本获得最新技术，而无需等待传统降价周期。

总之，苹果此次折扣活动罕见地针对新品展开，为消费者提供了实惠的购买机会。若用户正考虑购买新款MacBook Air，现在通过亚马逊渠道下单可避免错失良机。此外，对于追求更多硬件优惠的用户，新闻还建议参考相关PC硬件、存储、显示器等领域的交易页面，以获取更广泛的节省选项。

items.title: Intel reportedly in talks with Google and Amazon over advanced packaging — major customers could take advantage of EMIB-T later this year

Subscribe to our newsletter Intel is understood to be in active talks with Google and Amazon to provide advanced chip packaging services for their custom AI processors, according to a WIRED report published today, citing multiple sources. “Multiple sources say that Intel has been in ongoing talks with at least two large customers for its advanced packaging services: Google and Amazon,” claims the report.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThe deals, if closed, would represent a major influx of external revenue for Intel Foundry, which CFO Dave Zinsner said at the recent Morgan Stanley TMT conference is "close to closing some deals that are in the billions of dollars per year, in terms of revenue on packaging." Google, Amazon, and Intel all declined to comment on the specific customer relationships.Intel's advanced packaging portfolio centers on EMIB, a 2.5D technology that embeds small silicon bridges in the package substrate to connect chiplets, and Foveros, its 3D die-stacking process. The next-generation EMIB-T, which adds through-silicon vias to the bridge for improved power delivery and signal integrity, is set to roll out in production fabs this year. EMIB-T supports packages up to 120x180mm and can accommodate more than 38 bridges and over 12 reticle-sized dies.Article continues below You may like Apple and Nvidia considering Intel for 2028 chip production, report claims Intel shows off leading-edge tech with massive AI processor test vehicle AI chip design is pushing advanced chip packaging to its limits Intel is scaling capacity across three countries. Its Fab 9 facility in Rio Rancho, New Mexico, received $500 million from the CHIPS Act and has been operational since January 2024.The single-chip era is giving way to massive, interconnected systems. Intel's New Mexico advanced packaging fab is pushing the boundaries of what’s physically possible. By stacking chips using Foveros technology and interconnecting them with EMIB, @Intel_Foundry is scaling… pic.twitter.com/u54ezihfySApril 6, 2026In Malaysia, the Penang advanced packaging complex is 99% complete and will begin first-phase assembly and testing operations later this year, according to Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim, who confirmed the timeline after a briefing with Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan in March. Intel has also outsourced EMIB production for the first time to Amkor's Songdo K5 facility in South Korea, with additional sites planned in Portugal and Arizona.Naga Chandrasekaran, head of Intel Foundry, told WIRED that packaging has become more consequential than the silicon itself for AI computing going forward. “Even more so than the silicon itself, chip packaging is going to transform how this AI revolution comes to fruition over the next decade”, he said.Zinsner said at the January Q4 2025 earnings call that he had revised his packaging revenue projections over the previous 12 to 18 months from hundreds of millions of dollars to "well north of $1 billion." He added at the Morgan Stanley event that packaging could achieve the same 40% gross margins Intel claims on its core product business.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThose projections contrast sharply with the division's current financials. Intel Foundry posted $4.5 billion in revenue for Q4 2025 with a $2.5 billion operating loss. External foundry revenue for the full year totaled just $307 million, mostly from U.S. government contracts and residual Altera work. The Foundry division lost $10.3 billion on $17.8 billion in revenue for all of 2025, driven largely by the cost of ramping Intel 18A.Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: 据报道英特尔就先进封装与谷歌亚马逊谈判——主要客户群或于下半年采用EMIB-T
items.descriptionZh:
据《WIRED》今日报道，英特尔据信正与谷歌和亚马逊进行积极谈判，旨在为这两家科技巨头的定制人工智能（AI）处理器提供先进的芯片封装服务。报道援引多个消息来源称，英特尔已与至少两家大型客户就其先进封装服务进行了持续洽谈，这两家客户正是谷歌和亚马逊。若交易达成，将为英特尔代工服务（Intel Foundry）带来可观的外部收入。英特尔首席财务官戴夫·津斯纳在最近的摩根士丹利TMT会议上表示，该部门“接近达成几项交易，每年将带来数十亿美元的封装业务收入”。谷歌、亚马逊和英特尔均拒绝对具体的客户关系发表评论。

此次合作背景的核心在于，单一芯片时代正让位于由多个“小芯片”（Chiplet）互联组成的庞大系统。对于追求极致性能的AI处理器而言，如何高效、高带宽、低功耗地互连这些芯片，已成为继晶体管微缩之后的关键瓶颈。英特尔的先进封装技术正是瞄准了这一核心挑战。

**核心技术原理与创新点**
英特尔的先进封装产品组合主要围绕两项关键技术：
1.  **EMIB（嵌入式多芯片互连桥接）**：这是一种2.5D封装技术。其创新之处在于，并非使用一整块昂贵的硅中介层来连接小芯片，而是将微小的硅桥（约100微米厚）嵌入到常规的封装基板中。这些硅桥充当了高速互连通道，只在需要连接的芯片下方存在，从而实现了高密度互连，同时降低了复杂性和成本。
2.  **Foveros**：这是英特尔的3D芯片堆叠技术。它允许将不同工艺节点、不同功能（如计算、缓存、I/O）的芯片像搭积木一样垂直堆叠在一起，并通过微小的硅通孔（TSV）进行上下层间的直接电信号连接。这极大提升了芯片间带宽和能效，并实现了异质集成。

其下一代技术**EMIB-T**代表了进一步创新。它在原有的EMIB硅桥中加入了**硅通孔**，不仅用于连接并排的芯片，还能改善供电和信号完整性。这使得封装可以支持更大尺寸（达120x180mm）、容纳更多硅桥（超过38个）和更多光罩尺寸的大型芯片（超过12个），为构建超大规模的AI处理器奠定了基础。该技术预计将于今年在生产工厂中部署。

**产能布局与性能参数**
英特尔正在全球积极扩张其先进封装产能，以支持这些潜在的大规模订单：
*   **美国新墨西哥州**：Fab 9工厂已获得《芯片法案》5亿美元资助，并于2024年1月投入运营。
*   **马来西亚槟城**：先进封装综合设施已完成99%，将于今年晚些时候启动第一阶段组装和测试运营。
*   **韩国**：英特尔首次将部分EMIB生产外包给Amkor的Songdo K5工厂。
*   **未来规划**：葡萄牙和亚利桑那州的站点也在计划中。

**技术影响与财务前景**
英特尔代工服务负责人纳加·钱德拉塞卡兰指出，对于未来的AI计算，**芯片封装的重要性甚至可能超过硅芯片本身**，它将决定未来十年AI革命如何实现。
从财务角度看，先进封装被英特尔视为一个高增长、高利润率的业务。津斯纳已将未来12至18个月的封装收入预期从数亿美元上调至“远超10亿美元”，并认为该业务有望实现与英特尔核心产品业务相当的40%毛利率。

然而，这一乐观前景与英特尔代工部门当前的财务状况形成鲜明对比。该部门在2025年第四季度营收45亿美元，运营亏损25亿美元；2025年全年营收178亿美元，亏损高达103亿美元，主要受英特尔18A制程工艺投产爬坡的巨大成本拖累。全年仅3.07亿美元的外部代工收入（主要来自美国政府合同和残余的Altera业务），突显了获取谷歌、亚马逊这类顶级客户对英特尔代工战略转型的至关重要性。

总而言之，与谷歌和亚马逊的潜在合作，不仅是英特尔先进封装技术获得市场认可的重要里程碑，更是其代工业务扭转巨额亏损、实现长期增长蓝图的关键一步。这反映了在AI时代，从单一芯片设计到系统级封装与集成的竞争范式转移。

items.title: Amazon, Microsoft, and Google under investor pressure to disclose site-specific data center water and power consumption — more than a dozen shareholders ask for transparency ahead of annual investor meetings
items.description: More than a dozen investors are pressuring Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet's Google to provide detailed data on water and energy consumption at their U.S. data centers.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 13 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter More than a dozen investors are pressuring Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet's Google to provide detailed data on water and energy consumption at their U.S. data centers, Reuters reported today. The pressure comes as all three companies have recently scrapped multibillion-dollar data center projects following community opposition, and as North American data centers consumed nearly 1 trillion liters of water in 2025, according to market research firm Mordor Intelligence.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowTrillium Asset Management, a Boston-based firm that invests partly based on a company's environmental standards, filed a resolution with Alphabet in December seeking to understand how the company planned to meet climate goals it set in 2020, Andrea Ranger, director of shareholder advocacy at Trillium, told Reuters. Alphabet pledged six years ago to halve its emissions and shift to carbon-free energy sources by 2030, but Trillium said emissions instead rose 51%.Water usage is still a key concern for AI data centers. Some estimates say that generating just 100 words with OpenAI's GPT-4 consumes three bottles of water. Last year, another study suggested that AI data centers use more water annually than people drink bottled water, globally.Article continues below You may like Microsoft to overhaul AI data center building with community-first approach Microsoft built a ‘Community-First AI Infrastructure’ framework for its data center projects Data center developers building private natural gas 'Shadow Grid' power plants to sidestep strained grids All four major hyperscalers have adopted closed-loop cooling systems that use substantially less water than traditional evaporative methods, but the level of disclosure varies.Data center water consumption involves more than closed-loop cooling, as well. The majority of water a data center consumes is indirect; i.e. for generating the electricity the data center will use. A 2024 study found that data centers (in general, not specifically AI data centers) consumed about 800 billion liters of water indirectly. Another 2025 study found the vast majority of water consumption happens offsite an AI data center.Investors want site-level data to assess operational risks more accurately. "We haven't seen them disclosing enough about their water consumption (and the) impact on the local community," Jason Qi, lead technology analyst at Calvert Research and Management, told Reuters.Josh Weissman, director of infrastructure capacity delivery at Amazon, told Reuters the company is "increasingly disclosing site-specific water consumption data where we operate." An Amazon spokesperson added that the company was committed to being a "good neighbor" and was investing in efficiency and water reduction efforts. A Microsoft spokesperson said environmental sustainability was "a core value." Google declined to comment, and Meta did not respond to Reuters' request.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThe backdrop of this is the alarming rate of AI data center expansion. Last week, Bloomberg reported that nearly half of planned U.S. data center builds for 2026 have been delayed or canceled, largely due to infrastructure demands. Although there's a clear call from some investors for sustainability, there's also a question of practicality when new projects are being funded so quickly. The capital expenditure of companies like Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta has shot up amid the AI boom, even if some projects will never actually be completed. items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: 投资者施压亚马逊、微软和谷歌披露特定数据中心水电消耗数据——十多家股东要求在年度投资者会议前提高透明度
items.descriptionZh:
超过一打投资者正施压亚马逊、微软和Alphabet旗下谷歌，要求其提供美国数据中心水资源和能源消耗的详细数据。这一压力出现的背景是，这三家巨头近期均因社区反对而取消了数十亿美元的数据中心建设项目，同时根据市场研究机构Mordor Intelligence的数据，北美数据中心在2025年已消耗近1万亿升水。

波士顿投资公司Trillium Asset Management的股东倡导总监Andrea Ranger向路透社透露，该公司于去年12月向Alphabet提交了一份决议，要求其说明如何实现在2020年设定的气候目标。Alphabet六年前承诺到2030年将排放量减半并转向无碳能源，但Trillium指出其排放量反而上升了51%。

水资源使用仍是AI数据中心的关键担忧。有估计称，仅用OpenAI的GPT-4生成100个单词就会消耗三瓶水。去年另一项研究则指出，全球AI数据中心的年耗水量已超过瓶装水饮用总量。

尽管四大超大规模云服务商均已采用耗水量远低于传统蒸发冷却方式的闭环冷却系统，但其披露程度参差不齐。数据中心的耗水不仅限于冷却系统本身，更大比例是间接用水，即为数据中心供电的发电环节所消耗的水资源。一项2024年的研究发现，数据中心（泛指，非特指AI数据中心）间接消耗了约8000亿升水；2025年的另一项研究则指出，AI数据中心绝大部分的用水发生在设施场地之外。

投资者希望获得站点级别的数据以更准确地评估运营风险。Calvert Research and Management的首席科技分析师Jason Qi表示：“我们尚未看到它们（科技公司）充分披露其用水量以及对当地社区的影响。”

亚马逊基础设施容量交付总监Josh Weissman回应称，公司“正越来越多地披露我们在运营地的站点具体用水数据”。亚马逊发言人补充说，公司致力于成为“好邻居”，并正投资于提升能效和减少用水。微软发言人表示环境可持续性是“核心价值”。谷歌拒绝置评，Meta则未回应路透社的询问。

这一系列事件的背景是AI数据中心的惊人扩张速度。上周，彭博社报道称，美国近一半计划于2026年建设的数据中心项目已被延迟或取消，主要原因在于基础设施需求难以满足。尽管部分投资者明确呼吁可持续发展，但在新项目获得快速融资的现实中，也存在着实用性的考量。在AI热潮中，微软、亚马逊和Meta等公司的资本支出大幅飙升，尽管其中部分项目可能永远无法真正完工。

Subscribe to our newsletter A bipartisan group of U.S. senators has proposed a new law that would impose an almost blanked ban on exports of advanced wafer fabrication equipment (WFE) to select entities in adversary nations, a rule that would complement the existing fab-based bans. If imposed, leading China-based chipmakers like CXMT, Hua Hong, SMIC, and YMTC would lose the ability to procure advanced tools for outdated fabs and use them to advance their leading-edge facilities.Go deeper with TH Premium: Chipmaking (Image credit: tsmc)A deeper look at the chipmaking supply chainTSMC's $165 billion U.S. investments examinedChina reportedly reverse-engineers EUV toolChina bets on DUV, as EUV blockade reshapes chipmaking"Certain entities, including [CXMT, Hua Hong/HLMC, Huawei, SMIC, and YMTC] are engaged in efforts to produce advanced-node integrated circuits that are especially crucial for the Military-Civil Fusion efforts of the People’s Republic of China and warrant comprehensive export controls to prevent those companies from accessing items made with [U.S.] technologies," the proposal reads.The U.S. government restricted sales of advanced wafer fabrication equipment to potential foes like China back in late 2021. Under these restrictions American companies and eventually companies from allied nations, including the Netherlands and Japan, must obtain an export license from the U.S. government to ship WFE capable of 14nm process technologies for logic, 18nm-class DRAM fabrication processes, and 128-layer or more NAND if they ship to China-based entities. The new proposal does not introduce anything new in terms of curbs. What it does is change how shipments of tools are permitted.Article continues below You may like U.S. lawmakers demand sales ban on chipmaking tools to China China's top chip execs claim ASML alternative 'small, fragmented, and weak' SMIC sent chipmaking tools to Iran's military, Trump administration officials say Today export controls regulate shipments to exact fabs that may or may not be blacklisted by the U.S. government, not entities that own these fabs. To that end, WFE makers can ship advanced machines, such as the ASML Twinscan NXT:1950i/1980Di (which can be upgraded to the sanctioned 5nm-capable NXT:2000i) to companies like SMIC as long as the latter does not use these tools for advanced process technologies. However, controlling how these tools are used is extremely complicated as Chinese chipmakers and authorities do not really endorse such audits. Meanwhile, these advanced tools are indeed used to make chips using 7nm-class process technologies, such as SMIC's N+1 and N+2.The newly proposed MATCH Act shifts export controls from fab-based controls to a hybrid model that is company-based (and affiliation-based), though fab-level triggers will remain in place. As a result, companies like SMIC will not be able to buy advanced tools for fabs that use trailing nodes and then redirect them to their fabs capable of 7nm-class technologies.In addition, the MATCH Act is designed to force global alignment on semiconductor equipment export controls. At first, the U.S. will pursue coordination with allied supplier countries such as the Netherlands, Japan, South Korea, and Taiwan. If that effort fails, then the U.S. will expand restrictions extraterritorially to cover foreign-made tools that contain more than 0% of American technology, or require servicing that relies on American technologies. The end goal is to close loopholes that enable adversaries to circumvent existing controls.An avid reader who has been following U.S. restrictions on advanced fab tools will ask whether it will be possible for restricted China-based entities to get sophisticated WFE from various intermediaries that are not restricted. As far as we can tell, while intermediary entities can in theory acquire semiconductor manufacturing equipment and divert it illegally, the MATCH Act is explicitly structured to prevent circumvention via intermediaries as export controls attach not only to the initial transaction, but also to end-use, end-user, reexport, and servicing. Therefore, routing semiconductor equipment through a third-party entity would not bypass restrictions, but will instead expose all parties involved, which essentially means losing access to advanced WFE in the future and servicing of existing tools, which in turn will render these machines useless over time.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsWhile the bill cannot eliminate small-scale leakage of advanced systems, one-off tool diversion, or black market activity, it is designed to eliminate reliable supply chains currently used by Chinese entities to get advanced fabrication equipment to build up and maintain world-class fabs.Last but not least, the MATCH Act introduces the 75% threshold that serves as a built-in calibration mechanism that limits controls to genuine technological chokepoints that countries of concern (i.e., China) cannot produce themselves. For example, if China can meet 75% of its demand for certain tools (e.g., etching or deposition tools), then the U.S. government no longer restricts shipments of appropriate tools to China. Under the newly proposed bill, restrictions are applied where they retain strategic leverage and can be relaxed if domestic alternatives reach sufficient scale. This seems to be important for American companies that sell etching and deposition tools. For now, they maintain lead over Chinese makers like AMEC or Naura, but the latter already offers world-class tools, so once they reach scale, American companies like Applied Materials will lose strategic positions on the Chinese market, at which point the U.S. government will cease regulating their shipments to customers in the PRC. items.author: Anton Shilov
items.titleZh: 美国拟立法禁止对华出口DUV芯片制造及蚀刻设备
items.descriptionZh:
近日，美国两党参议员提出一项名为《MATCH法案》的新立法提案，旨在对向特定“对手国家”实体出口先进晶圆制造设备实施近乎全面的禁令，以弥补现有基于具体晶圆厂（fab-based）的出口管制漏洞。该法案若通过，长江存储、华虹集团、中芯国际、长鑫存储等中国领先芯片制造商将面临更严峻的挑战，可能无法再通过为旧产线采购先进设备，然后将其转移用于升级其先进制程产线。

**背景与现有管制框架**

美国政府自2021年底开始，已限制向中国等潜在对手国出售先进的晶圆制造设备。根据现行规定，美国公司及后续加入的荷兰、日本等盟友国家的公司，若向中国实体出口可用于14纳米以下逻辑芯片、18纳米级DRAM制造工艺或128层及以上NAND闪存生产的设备，必须获得美国政府颁发的出口许可证。然而，现有管制主要针对被列入黑名单的特定晶圆厂，而非拥有这些晶圆厂的公司实体。

这导致了一个明显的漏洞：只要不明确用于先进制程，设备制造商理论上可以将其先进设备出售给中国公司。例如，ASML的Twinscan NXT:1950i/1980i深紫外（DUV）光刻机（该机型可升级至受管制的5纳米级NXT:2000i型号）仍可出售给中芯国际等企业。但实践中，监督这些设备的实际用途极为困难，中国芯片制造商和当局并不真正支持此类审计。事实上，这些先进设备已被用于中芯国际的N+1、N+2等7纳米级工艺的生产。

**新法案的核心创新与管制原理**

新提出的《MATCH法案》关键创新在于将出口管制模式从“基于晶圆厂”转变为“基于公司实体”（及关联方）的混合模式，尽管晶圆厂层级的触发机制依然保留。这意味着，像中芯国际这样的公司，将无法再为其生产落后制程（trailing nodes）的晶圆厂采购先进设备，然后将其转运至其具备7纳米级技术能力的先进产线。

法案的核心逻辑是系统性“堵漏”：
1.  **扩大管制范围**：限制对象直接指向公司实体本身，而非具体某个厂房。只要该公司被列入清单，其所有关联实体购买先进设备都将受到限制。
2.  **强化全程追溯与责任**：法案明确规定，出口管制不仅适用于初始交易，还涵盖**最终用途、最终用户、再出口以及设备维护服务**。这意味着，即使通过未受限制的第三方中介进行采购和转运，也无法规避限制。相反，这种试图绕开管制的行为将使所有相关方（中介、最终用户等）面临严厉制裁，包括未来被切断所有先进设备的获取渠道以及现有设备的维护服务，从而使已购设备随时间推移而失效。
3.  **推动全球协同**：法案旨在迫使荷兰、日本、韩国、台湾等盟友国家和地区在半导体设备出口管制上与美国政策对齐。若协调失败，美国将启动“长臂管辖”，对任何包含超过**0%美国技术**的外国制造设备，或依赖美国技术进行维护的设备施加限制。最终目标是形成统一的国际管制阵线，彻底关闭对手规避现有控制的漏洞。

**技术影响与应用场景分析**

*   **对中国芯片制造业的直接影响**：包括中芯国际、长江存储、长鑫存储、华虹集团以及华为在内的主要中国芯片设计制造企业，其构建和维持世界级先进晶圆厂所需的可靠设备供应链将被系统性切断。法案虽无法完全杜绝小规模泄漏、一次性工具转移或黑市活动，但其目标是摧毁当前中国实体赖以获取先进制造设备、建设和维护尖端产能的稳定供应链体系。
*   **对全球设备供应商的影响**：法案引入了独特的 **“75%阈值”自校准机制**，成为其关键战略设计。该机制规定，如果中国自身能够满足其特定类型设备（如刻蚀机、沉积设备）需求的75%，那么美国政府将不再限制向中国出口该类设备。这实际上是将管制精准聚焦于中国尚无法自主替代的真正“技术瓶颈”环节。这一机制对美国企业（如应用材料公司）具有双重意义：一方面，在刻蚀、沉积等领域，他们目前仍领先于中微公司、北方华创等中国本土厂商，因此相关设备出口仍受管制；另一方面，一旦中国本土设备商（如中微、北方华创）的产能和市场份额达到75%的临界规模，美国政府将解除限制，届时美国公司在该市场的战略地位将下降，转而进入纯商业竞争环境。
*   **对全球供应链格局的长期影响**：法案旨在固化并强化美国及其盟友在半导体制造最尖端设备领域的“战略杠杆”。它通过复杂的法律和技术捆绑，试图长期延缓甚至阻滞中国在先进制程芯片制造领域的发展步伐，确保美国在关键技术领域的长期优势。这也会加速中国在半导体设备领域的自主研发和国产化替代进程。

**总结**

《MATCH法案》代表了美国对华半导体技术出口管制的一次重大升级和精细化管理尝试。它从管制对象、追溯机制和国际协同三个维度，系统性地构建了一套更严密、更难规避的封锁体系。其内置的“75%阈值”机制，更是将技术遏制与经济利益动态挂钩，意图在最大化战略遏制效果的同时，为本国产业保留商业空间。该法案的推进，预示着全球半导体产业的地缘政治分割将进一步加剧，中国半导体产业链的自主可控面临更为紧迫和复杂的挑战。

history.title: A new Anthropic model found security problems ‘in every major operating system and web browser’
history.description:
Anthropic is debuting a new AI model as part of a cybersecurity partnership with Nvidia, Google, Amazon Web Services, Apple, Microsoft, and other companies. Project Glasswing, as it's called, is billed as a way for large companies, and potentially even the government, to flag vulnerabilities in their systems with virtually no human intervention.
Anthropic is offering its launch partners access to Claude Mythos Preview, a new general-purpose model that it's not currently planning to publicly release due to security concerns. Newton Cheng, the cyber lead for Anthropic's frontier red team, told The Verge that the model will ideally give cyber  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: Anthropic is debuting a new AI model as part of a cybersecurity partnership with Nvidia, Google, Amazon Web Services, Apple, Microsoft, and other companies. Project Glasswing, as it’s called, is billed as a way for large companies, and potentially even the government, to flag vulnerabilities in their systems with virtually no human intervention.Anthropic is offering its launch partners access to Claude Mythos Preview, a new general-purpose model that it’s not currently planning to publicly release due to security concerns. Newton Cheng, the cyber lead for Anthropic’s frontier red team, told The Verge that the model will ideally give cyber defenders a “head start” against adversaries. The partners will use the model to analyze their system to spot high-stakes vulnerabilities and help patch them up. Access is restricted to keep those same adversaries from using it to find weak points and conduct attacks.Though Claude Mythos Preview wasn’t specifically trained for cybersecurity purposes, Anthropic said in a release that the model’s “strong agentic coding and reasoning skills” are behind its cybersecurity advances. In an interview with The Verge, Newton Cheng, the cyber lead for Anthropic’s frontier red team, declined to share specific details of the model’s cybersecurity successes beyond the company’s publicly-released examples, but Anthropic’s blog post said that in recent weeks, Mythos Preview has flagged “thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities, including some in every major operating system and web browser.” Anthropic’s blog post doesn’t mention keeping humans in the loop for the model’s cybersecurity sweeps; in fact, it highlights that the model identified vulnerabilities “and develop[ed] many related exploits — entirely autonomously, without any human steering.”Claude Mythos Preview’s existence was first reported last month in a data leak, which Anthropic attributes to human error. Dianne Penn, a head of product management at Anthropic, told The Verge in an interview that the company is “taking steps in terms of solidifying our processes … That was not related to software vulnerabilities in any way.”Mythos Preview will be privately available to the company’s Glasswing partners, which also include JPMorgan Chase, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, the Linux Foundation, and Palo Alto Networks, plus about 40 other organizations that maintain or build software infrastructure. For now, Anthropic will help subsidize the cost of using it. The company says it will commit up to $100 million in usage credits, plus $4 million in direct donations to the Linux Foundation and the Apache Software Foundation, said Cheng. In the long term, as Anthropic and other AI companies face pressure to turn a profit, the program could evolve into a paid service that provides a new revenue stream — if it works well enough for companies to keep using it.Despite its highly public recent clash with the Trump administration, Anthropic also said in the release that it has been in “ongoing discussions with US government officials about Claude Mythos Preview and its offensive and defensive cyber capabilities.” When The Verge asked what that meant, Penn confirmed that the company had “briefed senior officials in the US government about Mythos and what it can do,” and that the company is still “committed to working closely with all different levels of government.” Cheng said that though Anthropic is “engaged with” the government, he declined to speak to exactly who the company had briefed.
history.author: Hayden Field
history.titleZh: 新型Anthropic模型发现"每项主流操作系统及网络浏览器均存在安全隐患"
history.descriptionZh:
Anthropic联合Nvidia、谷歌、亚马逊云科技（AWS）、苹果、微软等科技巨头，共同推出一项名为“Project Glasswing”的网络安全合作项目。该项目旨在为大型企业乃至政府机构提供近乎无需人工干预的系统漏洞检测与修复能力。其核心是Anthropic最新开发的通用大模型——Claude Mythos Preview。由于安全考虑，该模型目前不计划公开发布，仅对项目合作伙伴开放。

在技术原理与创新性方面，Claude Mythos Preview并非专门为网络安全训练，但其核心优势在于强大的“智能体编码与推理能力”。正是这些通用能力使其在网络安全领域展现出卓越性能，能够自主分析复杂系统代码和架构，识别潜在漏洞。最大的创新点在于其高度的自主性：根据Anthropic官方博客，该模型不仅能“标记数千个高危漏洞”，还能“完全自主地开发相关的漏洞利用程序”，整个过程无需任何人工引导。这标志着AI在网络安全领域从辅助工具向自主智能体的重要演进。

关于性能表现，尽管Anthropic未披露详尽的基准测试对比数据，但其宣称的成果颇具说服力。在近期内部测试中，Mythos Preview已成功标记了“成千上万个高危漏洞，覆盖所有主流操作系统和网页浏览器”。这种在广泛而关键的数字基础设施中主动发现漏洞的能力，为防御方提供了宝贵的“先发优势”。项目合作伙伴（包括摩根大通、博通、思科、CrowdStrike、Linux基金会、Palo Alto Networks等约50家维护或构建软件基础设施的组织）将使用该模型来分析自身系统，定位高风险漏洞并协助修补。访问权限的严格限制，旨在防止相同的强大能力被对手用于发动攻击。

此次合作具有深远的技术与行业影响。首先，它将可能重塑企业安全运维模式，实现从被动响应到主动、持续、自动化漏洞猎杀的转变。其次，它开辟了AI模型在高度敏感、高风险的网络安全领域商业化应用的新路径。在应用场景上，该项目直接瞄准大型企业和关键政府部门的核心系统安全防护，这是网络安全需求最迫切、价值最高的领域。

值得注意的是，该模型的存在此前因一次“人为错误”导致的数据泄露而被曝光。Anthropic表示已加强内部流程。在商业层面，为推广此项目，Anthropic承诺提供高达1亿美元的使用额度补贴，并向Linux基金会和阿帕奇软件基金会直接捐赠400万美元。长远来看，如果模型效果获得市场持续认可，该项目有望演变为一项为Anthropic带来新收入的付费服务。

此外，尽管Anthropic近期与前特朗普政府存在公开分歧，但其在新闻稿中确认，已就Claude Mythos Preview的“进攻与防御网络能力”与美国政府官员进行“持续讨论”，并向高级官员进行了技术简报，表明了其与各级政府保持合作的意向。这凸显了此类尖端AI技术在国家安全层面的战略意义以及随之而来的治理挑战。

history.title: Intel will help build Elon Musk’s Terafab AI chip factory
history.description:
Elon Musk's Terafab AI chip project in Austin, Texas, is gaining a crucial new partner: Intel.
On Tuesday, the American chipmaker announced it was signing on to help design and build the sprawling facility, which would supply AI chips to Musk's two companies, SpaceX (newly merged with xAI) and Tesla. Musk needs AI chips to power his plans to build a "robot army" that includes self-driving cars and humanoid robots, as well as for the data centers he plans on launching into space. SpaceX plans on making its initial public offering later this year. 
"Terafab will close the gap between today's chip production and the future's demand - a future …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: Elon Musk’s Terafab AI chip project in Austin, Texas, is gaining a crucial new partner: Intel.On Tuesday, the American chipmaker announced it was signing on to help design and build the sprawling facility, which would supply AI chips to Musk’s two companies, SpaceX (newly merged with xAI) and Tesla. Musk needs AI chips to power his plans to build a “robot army” that includes self-driving cars and humanoid robots, as well as for the data centers he plans on launching into space. SpaceX plans on making its initial public offering later this year.“Terafab will close the gap between today’s chip production and the future’s demand - a future among the stars,” the Terafab website reads.The inclusion of Intel relieves some of the pressure on Musk to build the factory himself. In recent months, the billionaire has sounded increasingly desperate about his desire for someone to build a chip factory, or fab, to help fuel his AI ambitions. And he has questioned whether the chip-making industry can keep up with the demand. “Can someone else build these things?” Musk said during an earnings call earlier this year. “I mean, it’s very hard to build these things.”Building a chip fabrication plant is complex, requires billions of dollars, many years, and a ton of specialized equipment. Musk has experience building factories for cars and rockets, but not silicon.Now, it seems like the factory’s construction will fall on Intel, which is dealing with its own struggles. “Our ability to design, fabricate, and package ultra-high-performance chips at scale will help accelerate Terafab’s aim to produce 1 TW/year of compute to power future advances in AI and robotics,” the company said in a post on X today.Intel is currently building two fabs in Arizona, as part of a $20 billion investment to grow its US footprint. Taiwan’s TSMC is also planning an enormous “Gigafab” north of Phoenix, with plans to operate as many as 12 advanced semiconductor plants.
history.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
history.titleZh: 英特尔将助力建造埃隆·马斯克的Terafab人工智能芯片工厂
history.descriptionZh:
埃隆·马斯克位于得克萨斯州奥斯汀的Terafab人工智能芯片项目迎来关键合作伙伴——英特尔。本周二，这家美国芯片制造商宣布将参与该大型设施的设计与建造，该设施未来将为马斯克旗下两家公司（SpaceX与新合并的xAI）及特斯拉供应AI芯片。马斯克需要这些芯片驱动其包括自动驾驶汽车、人形机器人在内的“机器人军团”计划，以及计划部署至太空的数据中心。SpaceX预计今年下半年启动首次公开募股。

Terafab官网宣称其将“弥合当前芯片生产与未来星际时代需求之间的差距”。英特尔的加入缓解了马斯克自主建厂的压力。近几个月，这位亿万富翁多次公开表达对建造芯片工厂（fab）以支撑其AI野心的迫切需求，并质疑芯片行业能否跟上需求增速。他在年初财报电话会议上坦言：“有没有别人能造这些？建造这些东西真的非常困难。”

芯片制造厂建设极其复杂，需投入数十亿美元、耗时多年，并依赖大量特种设备。马斯克虽具备汽车和火箭工厂的建设经验，但半导体制造领域仍是空白。如今，工厂建设重任将落在正面临自身转型挑战的英特尔肩上。英特尔今日在X平台发文称：“我们大规模设计、制造和封装超高性能芯片的能力，将助力加速实现Terafab年产1太瓦（TW）算力的目标，以推动AI与机器人技术的未来进展。”

目前英特尔正在亚利桑那州建设两座芯片工厂，作为其200亿美元美国本土扩张计划的一部分。与此同时，中国台湾的台积电也计划在凤凰城北部建造巨型“Gigafab”园区，规划运营多达12座先进半导体工厂。这一合作标志着传统芯片巨头与新兴AI算力需求方的深度结合，或将重塑高端芯片制造格局。

history.title: Intel joins Elon Musk's TeraFab project — 'Intel is proud to join the Terafab project with SpaceX, xAI, and Tesla to help refactor silicon fab technology'
history.description: As Intel joins Elon Musk's TeraFab project, Lip-Bu Tan expects Elon Musk to reimagine the semiconductor industry.

Subscribe to our newsletter In an unexpected turn of events, Intel on Tuesday said that it had joined Elon Musk's TeraFab project. The announcement mentions Intel's ability to develop, produce, and package advanced processors in high volumes, which could help Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI to get enough compute performance for next-generation AI and robotics applications. However, the announcement made in an X post you can expand below does not reveal how exactly Intel will help TeraFab.Intel is proud to join the Terafab project with @SpaceX, @xAI, and @Tesla to help refactor silicon fab technology.Our ability to design, fabricate, and package ultra-high-performance chips at scale will help accelerate Terafab’s aim to produce 1 TW/year of compute to power… pic.twitter.com/2vUmXn0YhHApril 7, 2026"Intel is proud to join the Terafab project with SpaceX, xAI, and Tesla to help refactor silicon fab technology," a statement by Intel reads. "Our ability to design, fabricate, and package ultra-high-performance chips at scale will help accelerate Terafab's aim to produce 1 TW/year of compute to power future advances in AI and robotics. It was fun hosting Elon Musk at Intel this past weekend!"The announcement is not accompanied by any press releases or SEC filings, which raises questions about the framework of the collaboration between Intel and TeraFab, as well as any possible legal bindings. In fact, the post in X is deliberately written in a way that barely reveals any concrete details about the structure of the partnership.Article continues below You may like Elon Musk unveils $20 billion ‘TeraFab’ chip project to make chips, memory, and package processors all under one roof Elon Musk says his Terafab Project chipmaking venture will launch in seven days Analyzing Elon Musk's TeraFab — A step towards Tesla and SpaceX's partial vertical integration, or an unattainable dream? Officially, TeraFab is positioned as the "most epic chip-building effort ever" that is to combine "logic, memory and advanced packaging under one roof," which implies localized production in a massive facility. Furthermore, the company is hiring managers to build a greenfield semiconductor fabrication plant in Texas. By contrast, Intel's wording rather implies a virtual semiconductor production ecosystem, or even a consortium that involves chip design, manufacturing, and packaging at Intel and demand from Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI. How such a consortium would differ from a typical wafer supply agreement that large companies tend to have with their suppliers is something that is unclear at this point.Considering the fact that Elon Musk wants his TeraFab project to ramp up production of chips as soon as possible, a reasonable way to achieve this would be to build a coordinated supply chain with a capacity pool that includes its own facilities as well as facilities belonging to other chipmakers, such as Intel. If we were to speculate further, we would envision custom co-invested fabs or at least product lines that would use unified process technologies, thus offering Elon Musk's companies dual or even triple sourcing to satisfy their demands.In fact, Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI may even order Intel to develop custom silicon tailored specifically for their workloads, as Intel officially offers custom silicon development services, though this is speculation.But while the Intel – TeraFab collaboration announcement lacks details on how Elon Musk plans to produce chips that would consume one terawatt of power on an annual basis, the chief executive of Intel expects Elon Musk to reimagine the entire semiconductor industry."Elon has a proven track record of reimagining entire industries," Lip-Bu Tan wrote in his own X post. "This is exactly what is needed in semiconductor manufacturing today. Terafab represents a step change in how silicon logic, memory and packaging will get built in the future. Intel is proud to be a partner and work closely with Elon on this highly strategic project."Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: 英特尔加入马斯克TeraFab项目——"英特尔很自豪能与SpaceX、xAI和特斯拉共同参与Terafab项目，助力重构硅芯片制造技术"
history.descriptionZh:
在一项出人意料的进展中，英特尔于周二宣布已加入埃隆·马斯克主导的TeraFab项目。该声明强调了英特尔大规模设计、制造和封装先进处理器（芯片）的能力，此举或将助力特斯拉、SpaceX及xAI获得足够的计算性能，以推动下一代人工智能与机器人技术的发展。然而，这份通过社交媒体X发布的声明并未具体阐明英特尔将如何协助TeraFab。

英特尔在声明中表示：“我们很自豪能与SpaceX、xAI和特斯拉共同加入TeraFab项目，助力重构硅晶圆厂技术。我们大规模设计、制造和封装超高性能芯片的能力，将有助于加速TeraFab实现年产1太瓦（1万亿瓦）计算能力的目标，为人工智能和机器人技术的未来进步提供动力。”声明还提到，英特尔上周末接待了马斯克到访。

此次公告并未伴随任何新闻稿或美国证券交易委员会文件，这引发了外界对双方合作框架及潜在法律约束的疑问。事实上，该社交媒体帖文措辞谨慎，几乎未透露任何关于合作结构的具体细节。

根据官方定位，TeraFab被描述为“有史以来最宏大的芯片制造计划”，旨在将“逻辑芯片、存储芯片和先进封装集成在同一屋檐下”，这暗示了其计划在一个大型设施中进行本地化生产。该公司正在得克萨斯州招聘管理人员，以建设一座全新的半导体制造工厂（晶圆厂）。

与之相对，英特尔的措辞更倾向于暗示一个虚拟的半导体生产生态系统，甚至可能是一个涉及英特尔芯片设计、制造、封装以及特斯拉、SpaceX和xAI需求的联盟。这种模式与大型公司通常与其供应商签订的晶圆供应协议有何不同，目前尚不明确。

考虑到马斯克希望TeraFab项目尽快提升芯片产量，一个合理的实现途径是构建一个协调的供应链，其产能池既包括自有设施，也包括英特尔等其他芯片制造商的工厂。进一步推测，这可能涉及共同投资的定制化晶圆厂，或至少是采用统一工艺技术的产品线，从而为马斯克旗下公司提供双重甚至三重供应来源，以满足其需求。

实际上，特斯拉、SpaceX和xAI甚至可能要求英特尔为其特定工作负载开发定制化硅芯片，因为英特尔官方确实提供定制芯片设计服务，尽管这仍属推测。

尽管英特尔的合作公告未详述马斯克计划如何生产年耗电量高达1太瓦的芯片，但英特尔首席执行官对马斯克重塑整个半导体产业寄予厚望。他在其X帖子中写道：“埃隆拥有重塑整个行业的成功履历，而这正是当今半导体制造业所需要的。TeraFab代表了未来硅逻辑、内存和封装制造方式的跨越式变革。英特尔很自豪能成为合作伙伴，并与埃隆在这一高度战略性的项目上紧密合作。”

总体而言，此次合作标志着传统半导体巨头与新兴垂直整合科技巨头之间的一次重要联手，其核心目标是通过整合设计与制造资源，突破现有算力规模瓶颈，为下一代AI与自动化系统奠定硬件基础。具体合作形式与商业模式仍有待后续披露。

history.titleZh: 苹果新款M5芯片MacBook Air 13寸降价促销，最高立省150美元，现仅售949美元起——全系机型迎来迄今最大力度优惠
history.descriptionZh:
苹果公司近期凭借其产品发布取得市场关注，继Neo系列的成功后，现针对2026年款的13英寸M5 MacBook Air笔记本电脑推出全系折扣促销，为新上市不到一个月的机型提供了高达150美元的降价，在当前经济环境下显得尤为诱人。这些折扣主要通过亚马逊平台提供，而官方Apple Store仍维持原价销售，使得此次促销成为消费者入手新款MacBook Air的绝佳时机。

新款MacBook Air搭载苹果最新的M5芯片，这是其核心技术创新点。该芯片采用10核CPU和10核GPU架构，相比前代M4芯片在速度和整体性能上实现了渐进式提升，延续了苹果自研芯片在能效比和集成度方面的优势。此外，笔记本内置苹果N1无线芯片，支持最新的Wi-Fi 7和蓝牙6连接标准，确保高速无线传输和低延迟体验。其他硬件配置包括13.6英寸Liquid Retina显示屏、12MP中心舞台摄像头和Touch ID指纹识别，这些特性共同提升了设备的显示效果、视频通话质量和安全性。

在性能参数和对比数据方面，新闻详细列出了三种主要配置及其折扣详情。首先是顶配机型，配备24GB统一内存和1TB SSD存储，原价1499美元，现价1349.99美元，节省149.01美元；其次是中配机型，配备16GB统一内存和1TB SSD存储，原价1299美元，现价1149.99美元，同样节省149.01美元；最后是入门机型，配备16GB统一内存和512GB SSD存储，原价1099美元，现价949美元，直降150美元。这些折扣使得各配置机型的价格分别降低了约10%至14%，性价比显著提高。所有机型均采用统一的轻薄设计，统一内存架构优化了多任务处理能力，而SSD存储选项则满足不同用户对空间的需求。

从技术影响和应用场景来看，M5 MacBook Air的轻薄特性使其成为移动办公和日常娱乐的理想选择。M5芯片的性能提升，结合Wi-Fi 7的增强连接能力，可支持更高效的内容创作、编程开发和多媒体消费。折扣促销不仅降低了消费者的入门门槛，还可能刺激市场对苹果新品的早期采纳，尤其是在经济不确定性增加的背景下。对于计划升级设备的用户而言，现在入手能以更低成本获得最新技术，而无需等待传统降价周期。

总之，苹果此次折扣活动罕见地针对新品展开，为消费者提供了实惠的购买机会。若用户正考虑购买新款MacBook Air，现在通过亚马逊渠道下单可避免错失良机。此外，对于追求更多硬件优惠的用户，新闻还建议参考相关PC硬件、存储、显示器等领域的交易页面，以获取更广泛的节省选项。

history.title: Intel reportedly in talks with Google and Amazon over advanced packaging — major customers could take advantage of EMIB-T later this year
history.description: Intel is understood to be in active talks with Google and Amazon to provide advanced chip packaging services for their custom AI ASICs.

Subscribe to our newsletter Intel is understood to be in active talks with Google and Amazon to provide advanced chip packaging services for their custom AI processors, according to a WIRED report published today, citing multiple sources. “Multiple sources say that Intel has been in ongoing talks with at least two large customers for its advanced packaging services: Google and Amazon,” claims the report.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThe deals, if closed, would represent a major influx of external revenue for Intel Foundry, which CFO Dave Zinsner said at the recent Morgan Stanley TMT conference is "close to closing some deals that are in the billions of dollars per year, in terms of revenue on packaging." Google, Amazon, and Intel all declined to comment on the specific customer relationships.Intel's advanced packaging portfolio centers on EMIB, a 2.5D technology that embeds small silicon bridges in the package substrate to connect chiplets, and Foveros, its 3D die-stacking process. The next-generation EMIB-T, which adds through-silicon vias to the bridge for improved power delivery and signal integrity, is set to roll out in production fabs this year. EMIB-T supports packages up to 120x180mm and can accommodate more than 38 bridges and over 12 reticle-sized dies.Article continues below You may like Apple and Nvidia considering Intel for 2028 chip production, report claims Intel shows off leading-edge tech with massive AI processor test vehicle AI chip design is pushing advanced chip packaging to its limits Intel is scaling capacity across three countries. Its Fab 9 facility in Rio Rancho, New Mexico, received $500 million from the CHIPS Act and has been operational since January 2024.The single-chip era is giving way to massive, interconnected systems. Intel's New Mexico advanced packaging fab is pushing the boundaries of what’s physically possible. By stacking chips using Foveros technology and interconnecting them with EMIB, @Intel_Foundry is scaling… pic.twitter.com/u54ezihfySApril 6, 2026In Malaysia, the Penang advanced packaging complex is 99% complete and will begin first-phase assembly and testing operations later this year, according to Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim, who confirmed the timeline after a briefing with Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan in March. Intel has also outsourced EMIB production for the first time to Amkor's Songdo K5 facility in South Korea, with additional sites planned in Portugal and Arizona.Naga Chandrasekaran, head of Intel Foundry, told WIRED that packaging has become more consequential than the silicon itself for AI computing going forward. “Even more so than the silicon itself, chip packaging is going to transform how this AI revolution comes to fruition over the next decade”, he said.Zinsner said at the January Q4 2025 earnings call that he had revised his packaging revenue projections over the previous 12 to 18 months from hundreds of millions of dollars to "well north of $1 billion." He added at the Morgan Stanley event that packaging could achieve the same 40% gross margins Intel claims on its core product business.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThose projections contrast sharply with the division's current financials. Intel Foundry posted $4.5 billion in revenue for Q4 2025 with a $2.5 billion operating loss. External foundry revenue for the full year totaled just $307 million, mostly from U.S. government contracts and residual Altera work. The Foundry division lost $10.3 billion on $17.8 billion in revenue for all of 2025, driven largely by the cost of ramping Intel 18A.Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 据报道英特尔就先进封装与谷歌亚马逊谈判——主要客户群或于下半年采用EMIB-T
history.descriptionZh:
据《WIRED》今日报道，英特尔据信正与谷歌和亚马逊进行积极谈判，旨在为这两家科技巨头的定制人工智能（AI）处理器提供先进的芯片封装服务。报道援引多个消息来源称，英特尔已与至少两家大型客户就其先进封装服务进行了持续洽谈，这两家客户正是谷歌和亚马逊。若交易达成，将为英特尔代工服务（Intel Foundry）带来可观的外部收入。英特尔首席财务官戴夫·津斯纳在最近的摩根士丹利TMT会议上表示，该部门“接近达成几项交易，每年将带来数十亿美元的封装业务收入”。谷歌、亚马逊和英特尔均拒绝对具体的客户关系发表评论。

此次合作背景的核心在于，单一芯片时代正让位于由多个“小芯片”（Chiplet）互联组成的庞大系统。对于追求极致性能的AI处理器而言，如何高效、高带宽、低功耗地互连这些芯片，已成为继晶体管微缩之后的关键瓶颈。英特尔的先进封装技术正是瞄准了这一核心挑战。

**核心技术原理与创新点**
英特尔的先进封装产品组合主要围绕两项关键技术：
1.  **EMIB（嵌入式多芯片互连桥接）**：这是一种2.5D封装技术。其创新之处在于，并非使用一整块昂贵的硅中介层来连接小芯片，而是将微小的硅桥（约100微米厚）嵌入到常规的封装基板中。这些硅桥充当了高速互连通道，只在需要连接的芯片下方存在，从而实现了高密度互连，同时降低了复杂性和成本。
2.  **Foveros**：这是英特尔的3D芯片堆叠技术。它允许将不同工艺节点、不同功能（如计算、缓存、I/O）的芯片像搭积木一样垂直堆叠在一起，并通过微小的硅通孔（TSV）进行上下层间的直接电信号连接。这极大提升了芯片间带宽和能效，并实现了异质集成。

其下一代技术**EMIB-T**代表了进一步创新。它在原有的EMIB硅桥中加入了**硅通孔**，不仅用于连接并排的芯片，还能改善供电和信号完整性。这使得封装可以支持更大尺寸（达120x180mm）、容纳更多硅桥（超过38个）和更多光罩尺寸的大型芯片（超过12个），为构建超大规模的AI处理器奠定了基础。该技术预计将于今年在生产工厂中部署。

**产能布局与性能参数**
英特尔正在全球积极扩张其先进封装产能，以支持这些潜在的大规模订单：
*   **美国新墨西哥州**：Fab 9工厂已获得《芯片法案》5亿美元资助，并于2024年1月投入运营。
*   **马来西亚槟城**：先进封装综合设施已完成99%，将于今年晚些时候启动第一阶段组装和测试运营。
*   **韩国**：英特尔首次将部分EMIB生产外包给Amkor的Songdo K5工厂。
*   **未来规划**：葡萄牙和亚利桑那州的站点也在计划中。

**技术影响与财务前景**
英特尔代工服务负责人纳加·钱德拉塞卡兰指出，对于未来的AI计算，**芯片封装的重要性甚至可能超过硅芯片本身**，它将决定未来十年AI革命如何实现。
从财务角度看，先进封装被英特尔视为一个高增长、高利润率的业务。津斯纳已将未来12至18个月的封装收入预期从数亿美元上调至“远超10亿美元”，并认为该业务有望实现与英特尔核心产品业务相当的40%毛利率。

然而，这一乐观前景与英特尔代工部门当前的财务状况形成鲜明对比。该部门在2025年第四季度营收45亿美元，运营亏损25亿美元；2025年全年营收178亿美元，亏损高达103亿美元，主要受英特尔18A制程工艺投产爬坡的巨大成本拖累。全年仅3.07亿美元的外部代工收入（主要来自美国政府合同和残余的Altera业务），突显了获取谷歌、亚马逊这类顶级客户对英特尔代工战略转型的至关重要性。

总而言之，与谷歌和亚马逊的潜在合作，不仅是英特尔先进封装技术获得市场认可的重要里程碑，更是其代工业务扭转巨额亏损、实现长期增长蓝图的关键一步。这反映了在AI时代，从单一芯片设计到系统级封装与集成的竞争范式转移。

history.title: Amazon, Microsoft, and Google under investor pressure to disclose site-specific data center water and power consumption — more than a dozen shareholders ask for transparency ahead of annual investor meetings
history.description: More than a dozen investors are pressuring Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet's Google to provide detailed data on water and energy consumption at their U.S. data centers.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 13 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter More than a dozen investors are pressuring Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet's Google to provide detailed data on water and energy consumption at their U.S. data centers, Reuters reported today. The pressure comes as all three companies have recently scrapped multibillion-dollar data center projects following community opposition, and as North American data centers consumed nearly 1 trillion liters of water in 2025, according to market research firm Mordor Intelligence.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowTrillium Asset Management, a Boston-based firm that invests partly based on a company's environmental standards, filed a resolution with Alphabet in December seeking to understand how the company planned to meet climate goals it set in 2020, Andrea Ranger, director of shareholder advocacy at Trillium, told Reuters. Alphabet pledged six years ago to halve its emissions and shift to carbon-free energy sources by 2030, but Trillium said emissions instead rose 51%.Water usage is still a key concern for AI data centers. Some estimates say that generating just 100 words with OpenAI's GPT-4 consumes three bottles of water. Last year, another study suggested that AI data centers use more water annually than people drink bottled water, globally.Article continues below You may like Microsoft to overhaul AI data center building with community-first approach Microsoft built a ‘Community-First AI Infrastructure’ framework for its data center projects Data center developers building private natural gas 'Shadow Grid' power plants to sidestep strained grids All four major hyperscalers have adopted closed-loop cooling systems that use substantially less water than traditional evaporative methods, but the level of disclosure varies.Data center water consumption involves more than closed-loop cooling, as well. The majority of water a data center consumes is indirect; i.e. for generating the electricity the data center will use. A 2024 study found that data centers (in general, not specifically AI data centers) consumed about 800 billion liters of water indirectly. Another 2025 study found the vast majority of water consumption happens offsite an AI data center.Investors want site-level data to assess operational risks more accurately. "We haven't seen them disclosing enough about their water consumption (and the) impact on the local community," Jason Qi, lead technology analyst at Calvert Research and Management, told Reuters.Josh Weissman, director of infrastructure capacity delivery at Amazon, told Reuters the company is "increasingly disclosing site-specific water consumption data where we operate." An Amazon spokesperson added that the company was committed to being a "good neighbor" and was investing in efficiency and water reduction efforts. A Microsoft spokesperson said environmental sustainability was "a core value." Google declined to comment, and Meta did not respond to Reuters' request.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThe backdrop of this is the alarming rate of AI data center expansion. Last week, Bloomberg reported that nearly half of planned U.S. data center builds for 2026 have been delayed or canceled, largely due to infrastructure demands. Although there's a clear call from some investors for sustainability, there's also a question of practicality when new projects are being funded so quickly. The capital expenditure of companies like Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta has shot up amid the AI boom, even if some projects will never actually be completed. history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 投资者施压亚马逊、微软和谷歌披露特定数据中心水电消耗数据——十多家股东要求在年度投资者会议前提高透明度
history.descriptionZh:
超过一打投资者正施压亚马逊、微软和Alphabet旗下谷歌，要求其提供美国数据中心水资源和能源消耗的详细数据。这一压力出现的背景是，这三家巨头近期均因社区反对而取消了数十亿美元的数据中心建设项目，同时根据市场研究机构Mordor Intelligence的数据，北美数据中心在2025年已消耗近1万亿升水。

波士顿投资公司Trillium Asset Management的股东倡导总监Andrea Ranger向路透社透露，该公司于去年12月向Alphabet提交了一份决议，要求其说明如何实现在2020年设定的气候目标。Alphabet六年前承诺到2030年将排放量减半并转向无碳能源，但Trillium指出其排放量反而上升了51%。

水资源使用仍是AI数据中心的关键担忧。有估计称，仅用OpenAI的GPT-4生成100个单词就会消耗三瓶水。去年另一项研究则指出，全球AI数据中心的年耗水量已超过瓶装水饮用总量。

尽管四大超大规模云服务商均已采用耗水量远低于传统蒸发冷却方式的闭环冷却系统，但其披露程度参差不齐。数据中心的耗水不仅限于冷却系统本身，更大比例是间接用水，即为数据中心供电的发电环节所消耗的水资源。一项2024年的研究发现，数据中心（泛指，非特指AI数据中心）间接消耗了约8000亿升水；2025年的另一项研究则指出，AI数据中心绝大部分的用水发生在设施场地之外。

投资者希望获得站点级别的数据以更准确地评估运营风险。Calvert Research and Management的首席科技分析师Jason Qi表示：“我们尚未看到它们（科技公司）充分披露其用水量以及对当地社区的影响。”

亚马逊基础设施容量交付总监Josh Weissman回应称，公司“正越来越多地披露我们在运营地的站点具体用水数据”。亚马逊发言人补充说，公司致力于成为“好邻居”，并正投资于提升能效和减少用水。微软发言人表示环境可持续性是“核心价值”。谷歌拒绝置评，Meta则未回应路透社的询问。

这一系列事件的背景是AI数据中心的惊人扩张速度。上周，彭博社报道称，美国近一半计划于2026年建设的数据中心项目已被延迟或取消，主要原因在于基础设施需求难以满足。尽管部分投资者明确呼吁可持续发展，但在新项目获得快速融资的现实中，也存在着实用性的考量。在AI热潮中，微软、亚马逊和Meta等公司的资本支出大幅飙升，尽管其中部分项目可能永远无法真正完工。

history.title: US lawmakers aim to ban export of DUV chipmaking and etching tools to leading firms in China — bipartisan proposal would ban lithography equipment for Huawei, SMIC, and others
history.description: Bipartisan group of U.S. senators propose to impose blanket ban on export of advanced DUV lithography and etching tools to Chinese companies known to have worked with China's military, such as CXMT, Huawei, SMIC, and YMTC.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 8 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter A bipartisan group of U.S. senators has proposed a new law that would impose an almost blanked ban on exports of advanced wafer fabrication equipment (WFE) to select entities in adversary nations, a rule that would complement the existing fab-based bans. If imposed, leading China-based chipmakers like CXMT, Hua Hong, SMIC, and YMTC would lose the ability to procure advanced tools for outdated fabs and use them to advance their leading-edge facilities.Go deeper with TH Premium: Chipmaking (Image credit: tsmc)A deeper look at the chipmaking supply chainTSMC's $165 billion U.S. investments examinedChina reportedly reverse-engineers EUV toolChina bets on DUV, as EUV blockade reshapes chipmaking"Certain entities, including [CXMT, Hua Hong/HLMC, Huawei, SMIC, and YMTC] are engaged in efforts to produce advanced-node integrated circuits that are especially crucial for the Military-Civil Fusion efforts of the People’s Republic of China and warrant comprehensive export controls to prevent those companies from accessing items made with [U.S.] technologies," the proposal reads.The U.S. government restricted sales of advanced wafer fabrication equipment to potential foes like China back in late 2021. Under these restrictions American companies and eventually companies from allied nations, including the Netherlands and Japan, must obtain an export license from the U.S. government to ship WFE capable of 14nm process technologies for logic, 18nm-class DRAM fabrication processes, and 128-layer or more NAND if they ship to China-based entities. The new proposal does not introduce anything new in terms of curbs. What it does is change how shipments of tools are permitted.Article continues below You may like U.S. lawmakers demand sales ban on chipmaking tools to China China's top chip execs claim ASML alternative 'small, fragmented, and weak' SMIC sent chipmaking tools to Iran's military, Trump administration officials say Today export controls regulate shipments to exact fabs that may or may not be blacklisted by the U.S. government, not entities that own these fabs. To that end, WFE makers can ship advanced machines, such as the ASML Twinscan NXT:1950i/1980Di (which can be upgraded to the sanctioned 5nm-capable NXT:2000i) to companies like SMIC as long as the latter does not use these tools for advanced process technologies. However, controlling how these tools are used is extremely complicated as Chinese chipmakers and authorities do not really endorse such audits. Meanwhile, these advanced tools are indeed used to make chips using 7nm-class process technologies, such as SMIC's N+1 and N+2.The newly proposed MATCH Act shifts export controls from fab-based controls to a hybrid model that is company-based (and affiliation-based), though fab-level triggers will remain in place. As a result, companies like SMIC will not be able to buy advanced tools for fabs that use trailing nodes and then redirect them to their fabs capable of 7nm-class technologies.In addition, the MATCH Act is designed to force global alignment on semiconductor equipment export controls. At first, the U.S. will pursue coordination with allied supplier countries such as the Netherlands, Japan, South Korea, and Taiwan. If that effort fails, then the U.S. will expand restrictions extraterritorially to cover foreign-made tools that contain more than 0% of American technology, or require servicing that relies on American technologies. The end goal is to close loopholes that enable adversaries to circumvent existing controls.An avid reader who has been following U.S. restrictions on advanced fab tools will ask whether it will be possible for restricted China-based entities to get sophisticated WFE from various intermediaries that are not restricted. As far as we can tell, while intermediary entities can in theory acquire semiconductor manufacturing equipment and divert it illegally, the MATCH Act is explicitly structured to prevent circumvention via intermediaries as export controls attach not only to the initial transaction, but also to end-use, end-user, reexport, and servicing. Therefore, routing semiconductor equipment through a third-party entity would not bypass restrictions, but will instead expose all parties involved, which essentially means losing access to advanced WFE in the future and servicing of existing tools, which in turn will render these machines useless over time.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsWhile the bill cannot eliminate small-scale leakage of advanced systems, one-off tool diversion, or black market activity, it is designed to eliminate reliable supply chains currently used by Chinese entities to get advanced fabrication equipment to build up and maintain world-class fabs.Last but not least, the MATCH Act introduces the 75% threshold that serves as a built-in calibration mechanism that limits controls to genuine technological chokepoints that countries of concern (i.e., China) cannot produce themselves. For example, if China can meet 75% of its demand for certain tools (e.g., etching or deposition tools), then the U.S. government no longer restricts shipments of appropriate tools to China. Under the newly proposed bill, restrictions are applied where they retain strategic leverage and can be relaxed if domestic alternatives reach sufficient scale. This seems to be important for American companies that sell etching and deposition tools. For now, they maintain lead over Chinese makers like AMEC or Naura, but the latter already offers world-class tools, so once they reach scale, American companies like Applied Materials will lose strategic positions on the Chinese market, at which point the U.S. government will cease regulating their shipments to customers in the PRC. history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: 美国拟立法禁止对华出口DUV芯片制造及蚀刻设备
history.descriptionZh:
近日，美国两党参议员提出一项名为《MATCH法案》的新立法提案，旨在对向特定“对手国家”实体出口先进晶圆制造设备实施近乎全面的禁令，以弥补现有基于具体晶圆厂（fab-based）的出口管制漏洞。该法案若通过，长江存储、华虹集团、中芯国际、长鑫存储等中国领先芯片制造商将面临更严峻的挑战，可能无法再通过为旧产线采购先进设备，然后将其转移用于升级其先进制程产线。

**背景与现有管制框架**

美国政府自2021年底开始，已限制向中国等潜在对手国出售先进的晶圆制造设备。根据现行规定，美国公司及后续加入的荷兰、日本等盟友国家的公司，若向中国实体出口可用于14纳米以下逻辑芯片、18纳米级DRAM制造工艺或128层及以上NAND闪存生产的设备，必须获得美国政府颁发的出口许可证。然而，现有管制主要针对被列入黑名单的特定晶圆厂，而非拥有这些晶圆厂的公司实体。

这导致了一个明显的漏洞：只要不明确用于先进制程，设备制造商理论上可以将其先进设备出售给中国公司。例如，ASML的Twinscan NXT:1950i/1980i深紫外（DUV）光刻机（该机型可升级至受管制的5纳米级NXT:2000i型号）仍可出售给中芯国际等企业。但实践中，监督这些设备的实际用途极为困难，中国芯片制造商和当局并不真正支持此类审计。事实上，这些先进设备已被用于中芯国际的N+1、N+2等7纳米级工艺的生产。

**新法案的核心创新与管制原理**

新提出的《MATCH法案》关键创新在于将出口管制模式从“基于晶圆厂”转变为“基于公司实体”（及关联方）的混合模式，尽管晶圆厂层级的触发机制依然保留。这意味着，像中芯国际这样的公司，将无法再为其生产落后制程（trailing nodes）的晶圆厂采购先进设备，然后将其转运至其具备7纳米级技术能力的先进产线。

法案的核心逻辑是系统性“堵漏”：
1.  **扩大管制范围**：限制对象直接指向公司实体本身，而非具体某个厂房。只要该公司被列入清单，其所有关联实体购买先进设备都将受到限制。
2.  **强化全程追溯与责任**：法案明确规定，出口管制不仅适用于初始交易，还涵盖**最终用途、最终用户、再出口以及设备维护服务**。这意味着，即使通过未受限制的第三方中介进行采购和转运，也无法规避限制。相反，这种试图绕开管制的行为将使所有相关方（中介、最终用户等）面临严厉制裁，包括未来被切断所有先进设备的获取渠道以及现有设备的维护服务，从而使已购设备随时间推移而失效。
3.  **推动全球协同**：法案旨在迫使荷兰、日本、韩国、台湾等盟友国家和地区在半导体设备出口管制上与美国政策对齐。若协调失败，美国将启动“长臂管辖”，对任何包含超过**0%美国技术**的外国制造设备，或依赖美国技术进行维护的设备施加限制。最终目标是形成统一的国际管制阵线，彻底关闭对手规避现有控制的漏洞。

**技术影响与应用场景分析**

*   **对中国芯片制造业的直接影响**：包括中芯国际、长江存储、长鑫存储、华虹集团以及华为在内的主要中国芯片设计制造企业，其构建和维持世界级先进晶圆厂所需的可靠设备供应链将被系统性切断。法案虽无法完全杜绝小规模泄漏、一次性工具转移或黑市活动，但其目标是摧毁当前中国实体赖以获取先进制造设备、建设和维护尖端产能的稳定供应链体系。
*   **对全球设备供应商的影响**：法案引入了独特的 **“75%阈值”自校准机制**，成为其关键战略设计。该机制规定，如果中国自身能够满足其特定类型设备（如刻蚀机、沉积设备）需求的75%，那么美国政府将不再限制向中国出口该类设备。这实际上是将管制精准聚焦于中国尚无法自主替代的真正“技术瓶颈”环节。这一机制对美国企业（如应用材料公司）具有双重意义：一方面，在刻蚀、沉积等领域，他们目前仍领先于中微公司、北方华创等中国本土厂商，因此相关设备出口仍受管制；另一方面，一旦中国本土设备商（如中微、北方华创）的产能和市场份额达到75%的临界规模，美国政府将解除限制，届时美国公司在该市场的战略地位将下降，转而进入纯商业竞争环境。
*   **对全球供应链格局的长期影响**：法案旨在固化并强化美国及其盟友在半导体制造最尖端设备领域的“战略杠杆”。它通过复杂的法律和技术捆绑，试图长期延缓甚至阻滞中国在先进制程芯片制造领域的发展步伐，确保美国在关键技术领域的长期优势。这也会加速中国在半导体设备领域的自主研发和国产化替代进程。

**总结**

《MATCH法案》代表了美国对华半导体技术出口管制的一次重大升级和精细化管理尝试。它从管制对象、追溯机制和国际协同三个维度，系统性地构建了一套更严密、更难规避的封锁体系。其内置的“75%阈值”机制，更是将技术遏制与经济利益动态挂钩，意图在最大化战略遏制效果的同时，为本国产业保留商业空间。该法案的推进，预示着全球半导体产业的地缘政治分割将进一步加剧，中国半导体产业链的自主可控面临更为紧迫和复杂的挑战。
